Title: Identification of bottlenecks in the accumulation of cyclic fatty acids in camelina seed oil

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial feedstock for coatings, lubricants, plastics and cosmetics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil; phosphatidylcholine (PC); phosphatidylethanolamine (PE); diacylglycerol (DAG); triacylglycerol (TAG); embryo axis and cotyledon
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cyclic fatty acid / cyclopropane fatty acid biosynthesis within seed lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: modified fatty acid formation on membrane phospholipids, especially PC and PE, feeding into DAG/TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts oleic acid (18:1) esterified in phospholipids into cyclopropane fatty acid (19:0 CPA)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream entry step for CPA formation on phospholipids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enabling step for CPA production, but downstream transfer from PC to DAG and DAG to TAG are the major bottlenecks
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables CPA accumulation up to about 10% of total seed fatty acids alone and >15% when co-expressed with SfLPAT; causes CPA to accumulate strongly in PC and PE but less efficiently in DAG and TAG
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in seeds under a seed-specific phaseolin promoter; similar expression in embryo axis and cotyledon
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development under transgenic expression in camelina grown at 22 °C, 16-h photoperiod
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic seed-specific expression; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; qRT-PCR; lipid class separation; stereospecific analysis of PC/PE/DAG/TAG; ESI-MS/MS; MALDI-MSI
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by LC/MS and MALDI/MSI; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: minor decrease (<10%) in seed weight in most lines; mild delay in seedling establishment/growth at high CPA; no substantial reduction in total seed oil or germination in camelina
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong core enzyme for engineering CPA production in oilseed crops; should be stacked with compatible acyltransferases to improve flux from PC to DAG and DAG to TAG
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among four tested CPS genes, only EcCPS produced detectable CPA in camelina seeds. 2. EcCPS caused CPA to accumulate to >60% in PC and PE but much lower levels in DAG and TAG, revealing downstream bottlenecks. 3. EcCPS-derived CPA was enriched at sn-1 of PC and PE and was heterogeneously distributed, with higher accumulation in embryo axis than cotyledon.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Plant CPS genes GhCPS1, GhCPS2 and SfCPS were tested but produced no detectable CPA in camelina, so EcCPS was the only effective CPS in this system. EcCPS also yielded elongated 21:0 and 23:0 CPA and trace desaturated 19:1 CPA products in camelina.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial feedstock for coatings, lubricants, plastics and cosmetics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil; especially increased DAG and TAG pools; also increases di-CPA-PC
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: triacylglycerol assembly / modified fatty acid incorporation into glycerolipids
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Kennedy pathway acylation step and CPA-enriched acyl editing cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates lysophosphatidic acid using acyl-CoA, with preference consistent with CPA-CoA/CPA-LPA utilization, thereby increasing di-CPA phospholipid and downstream neutral lipid formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: lysophosphatidic acid plus acyl-CoA, including CPA-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: phosphatidic acid and enhanced di-CPA-containing glycerolipid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream acyltransfer step affecting flux of CPA into storage lipids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool and promotes transfer of target product into DAG/TAG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a bottleneck-relieving enzyme for CPA incorporation, not sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: co-expression with EcCPS increased seed CPA by up to 90% relative to EcCPS alone, doubled CPA in DAG and TAG, and increased di-CPA-PC proportion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in seeds under seed-specific phaseolin promoter; similar expression in embryo axis and cotyledon
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development under transgenic co-expression in camelina grown at 22 °C, 16-h photoperiod
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression with EcCPS in transgenic camelina; qRT-PCR; GC-MS; stereospecific lipid analysis; ESI-MS/MS; MALDI-MSI
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by LC/MS and MALDI/MSI; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: slight decrease in CPA in T4 compared with T3 in some lines; mild delay in germination/early growth at high CPA but no major oil or yield penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable stacking partner with EcCPS to relieve bottlenecks in unusual fatty acid incorporation into seed oil; supports use of source-species acyltransferases for engineering modified oils
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of SfLPAT with EcCPS raised seed CPA accumulation to >15% and up to nearly 20% in camelina seeds. 2. SfLPAT increased the proportion of di-CPA-PC and redistributed CPA toward the sn-2 position in PC and PE. 3. This was associated with marked increases in CPA-DAG and CPA-TAG, indicating improved flux of CPA into storage oil.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers SfLPAT preference for CPA-LPA and CPA-CoA and a CPA acyl exchange cycle. Its effect was metabolic rather than transcriptional, because EcCPS transcript levels were not higher in co-expression lines.
